mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or 20d forward returns are negative in >55% of co-movement instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a rising industrial production index reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles are followed by negative 5d returns in >60% of cases, or the regime filter eliminates all predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 20d forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 60d returns in >65% of observations, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated, the combination signals stagflationary pressure that suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05, or 60d returns are positive in >50% of high-rate/low-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production alongside stable unemployment indicates broad-based economic normalization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 20d returns in >55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy outside the specified regime.
